name: South Africa
intro: South Africa has the continent's most mature project profession: banks and insurers in Johannesburg and Cape Town run large transformation portfolios, mining houses execute capital programmes at global scale, and the energy crisis has spawned a wave of renewable and grid projects that hire formally certified managers. South African employers know international credentials well — PMP is written into many corporate role profiles, and Scrum certifications dominate the fintech and digital-banking scene. Our live online weekend batch runs on the IST schedule, converting to comfortable mid-morning to evening South African time. Instruction is in English with an international cohort, and invoices support employer skills-development spending. Afterwards we guide your exam application, provide full-length mock exams, and keep the trainer available for questions until you pass.

faq.q: How much does certification training cost in South Africa?
faq.a: The course fee is US$1,295 for PMP and US$695 for CSM, covering the full live online weekend workshop, study materials, mock exams and exam application support. PMI's PMP exam fee (US$425 member / US$675 non-member) is paid separately to PMI; the CSM exam is included in the course fee.

faq.q: Are the classes live or recorded?
faq.a: Every batch is taught live by an instructor over two weekend days — not pre-recorded videos. You can ask questions in real time, work through exercises with the cohort, and reach the trainer afterwards for doubts.

faq.q: Is the certification recognised by employers in South Africa?
faq.a: Yes. PMP (from PMI) and CSM (from Scrum Alliance) are global credentials recognised identically everywhere, including by employers and recruiters across South Africa.
